compiled_state.title: As a project maintainer, I can rely on ci-llm-review-runner.sh auto-detecting whether to read DSO assets from a local checkout or a fetched directory
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Add auto-detection logic to ci-llm-review-runner.sh so it reads agent files and helper scripts from the local checkout when running inside the DSO plugin's own repo, and from the fetched assets directory ($DSO_ASSETS_DIR set by Story 4's fetch step) when running in a host-project CI environment.

**Why**: The DSO plugin's own CI must not pay the fetch overhead — its checkout already has plugins/dso/. Host-project CI must fetch. The runner needs a reliable, non-fragile signal to distinguish the two.

**Scope**:
- IN: detection logic that recognizes plugin self-CI via a stable marker (presence of a marker file like plugins/dso/.dso-source-of-truth at $REPO_ROOT, NOT a bare existence check on plugins/dso/agents/); fallback to $DSO_ASSETS_DIR when the marker is absent; tests for both code paths.
- OUT: the fetch step itself (Story 4); the runner script itself (Story 1).

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, ci-llm-review-runner.sh detects when running inside the DSO plugin's own repo (via marker file plugins/dso/.dso-source-of-truth at $REPO_ROOT) and reads agent files + helper scripts from the local checkout.
  ← Satisfies: "ci-llm-review-runner.sh auto-detects mode: when plugins/dso/agents/ is present at $REPO_ROOT it reads from local checkout"
- When this story is complete, in host-project CI (no marker file), the runner reads from $DSO_ASSETS_DIR (set by Story 4's fetch step).
  ← Satisfies: "else reads from $DSO_ASSETS_DIR (env var set by the fetch step)"
- When this story is complete, both code paths are covered by tests — one test exercising the local-checkout branch, one test exercising the fetched-assets branch.
  ← Satisfies: "Both paths covered by tests"
- unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Implicit-shared-state] The marker file plugins/dso/.dso-source-of-truth must be created in this story (committed to the DSO plugin repo) so the detection signal is reliable. A bare existence check on plugins/dso/agents/ is fragile — host projects may have stale vendored copies, partial fetches, or incidental files at that path. The marker file is the canonical source-of-truth signal.
- [Maintainability] Detection logic should be a single helper function in ci-llm-review-runner.sh (e.g., resolve_assets_dir()) so the auto-detect contract is documented in one place.

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
